_provenance: Promoted 2026-05-17 from godot/data/_drafts/ch1_cula_2026-05-17.json (Ch1 sweep). Transforms applied: (1) family_photo_ch1 + family_photo_ch1_repeat preserved at top for the canonical interactable dispatch; (2) internal-tagged Cula lines converted to speaker:'cula_internal' (registered in character_registry.json); (3) client_meeting_stance write_path removed from cula_b8_approach_choice (Halina owns; stance commit lives in halina.json::client_meeting_intro); (4) seven dwell trios collapsed into options blocks with chapter1.state_choice as the discriminator + reply states (Piotr's choice (a)).
_authoring_note: Cula's dialogue file is dispatched ONLY by the family-photo interactable (Signals.dialogue_requested('cula', ...) from family_photo_interactable nodes). **2026-05-25 fan-out (per critiques/2026-05-24-f4-fanout-recon.md):** 44 states migrated into NPC files (pig.json, murrow.json, crab.json, whimsy.json, halina.json, postcard_swine_ch1.json) as inline `{"speaker":"cula"}` slots or scaffolded reply states with TODO placeholders Piotr authors per the 'you author, I wire' decision. The 25 states remaining here are deferred to three NO_DISPATCH buckets: (1) ambient cula_internal observations + b8 internal_fee reactions await the per-zone ambient trigger sprint; (2) Beat 9 archive states await the Archive Room scene + Murrow archive-state-machine sprint; (3) Beat 12 court-round responses + Beat 13 internal/silent states await the court-round dispatch sprint. This file remains the writer's voice-doc consolidated reference for those deferred states; per the 2026-05-25 decision (Q4 of the fan-out questions), the file does not split into a separate _drafts/cula_deferred.json — voice continuity stays in one place.

lines: A family photo. Mr. Pig appears to be the youngest in it.

states._comment_ch4_scope: TODO: Chapter-4 retraversal focal observation is out of scope for this file (V1.1 pass 2 explicitly notes 'chapter-4 retraversal is out of scope'). Wire a ch4-triggered variant here when V1.4 content is authored. silent:true is the deliberate-empty-state marker per _schema.md §Silent states — replaces the legacy no-lines-means-fallback trick so the validator can distinguish missing content from intentional silence.

states._comment: Beat 1 — Office Street arrival. Cula speaks to himself before entering the firm. Address form n/a (no NPC). Dry-observer register; the doctorate joke is in the precision of his own self-irony (he does not boast — he flinches at the contrast between university and the doorway in front of him). One line. No options. The state is a barker for the door interactable; the existing cula.json idle_flavor already carries the office-street line — this state echoes it as a triggered observation so the player gets one Cula moment before the firm.
lines: Pig & Swine. Attorneys, counselors, possibly open. That is not how they described it at university.
states.speaker: cula_internal

states._comment: Beat 2 — entry into Pig & Swine. Cula sees Asia at the desk, three file piles, the case board, Mr. Pig pacing somewhere in the back. He has not yet spoken. The internal reaction is observational and short. Address form n/a (internal). Comedy is in the precise inventory landing on 'a noise that sounds admissible' — a sideways reference to the canonical coffee-machine line; the joke is that he registers it as evidence before registering it as a coffee machine. Two lines, the second tighter.
lines: Three file piles, one phone, one secretary, one panic from the back room.
Somewhere in here there is a coffee machine making a noise I cannot yet classify.
states.speaker: cula_internal

states._comment: Beat 8 setup — Cula returns to the office with Crab and Whimsy. Asia announces Halina. Internal-register: he registers the meeting room (formal, big table, contrast with the chaotic open office) without commenting. Stress register: short. One line. No options.
lines: The meeting room has a table the rest of the office cannot afford. That tells me something about how often it is used.
states.speaker: cula_internal

states._comment: Beat 8 sympathetic carrier — Cula's internal reaction at the fee moment. Per chapter_1.md §200: sympathetic stance registers the figure as heavy for a pensioner. One line, observational, no editorial. The 'pensioner' word is not in his mouth (would punch the line); he uses 'her month' which is concrete and human. Tagged 'internal'; speaker:'cula' to render the line as Cula's thought when a runner channel exists. No NPC reply expected.
states.speaker: cula_internal
lines: Five thousand. That is most of her month.

states._comment: Beat 8 blunt-procedural carrier — internal reaction at fee moment. Per chapter_1.md §201: registers the fee as a clean transaction. One line, procedural. The dryness IS the carrier. Tagged 'internal'.
states.speaker: cula_internal
lines: Fixed fee, no retainer, paid before court. Clean.

states._comment: Beat 8 technical carrier — internal reaction at fee moment. Per chapter_1.md §202: registers fee structure (single fixed, no retainer). One line, structural. Tagged 'internal'.
states.speaker: cula_internal
lines: Single payment, no instalments, no retainer. That is the cleanest fee shape this firm has billed in a while.

states._comment: Post-court internal reflection — incapacity decoy path only. Fires once after court_won_procedural_reset on the bench-initiative outcome. Cula registers the gap between what he argued and what the bench found. One line; no self-pity, no resolution. STAGING NOTE: speaker cula_internal has no rendered channel until the DialogueRunner thought-balloon extension lands (D3 = option c, per 2026-05-26 design plan Open Decisions). This state is authored and staged; dispatch wiring is deferred to the AG sprint that builds the thought-balloon UI. on_dismiss writes incapacity_reflection_seen so the state fires at most once per playthrough.
states.speaker: cula_internal
lines: I held the wrong argument. The court found the right one without me.

states._comment: Beat 9 setup — Cula and Murrow at the Archive Room. Murrow opens the procedural binder to Article 135-bis. Cula's internal reaction is brief. The legal-argument register lifts (sentences lengthen, nervousness drops). One line; the article number lands.
lines: Article 135-bis, paragraph two. The binder has been waiting for this clause all morning.

states._comment: Beat 9 — after Murrow's first-clause narration (invalid-service definition, naming the 2015 renumbering and the landlord's knowledge). Cula's response is short, procedural; he names the structural fit. Address form: 'Murrow'. Sentences slightly longer on legal ground. Two lines.
lines: So the knowledge requirement is met. The landlord authorised the renumbering; the landlord cannot now claim ignorance of the address that resulted.
Murrow. That is half the work done before we wrote anything.

states._comment: Beat 9 — after Murrow's second-clause narration (motion + 14-day window, the actual-notice clock). Cula does the arithmetic out loud — the listing pattern from voice_agents/cula.md §Patterns. He does NOT panic at the tightness; he files it. One line plus a clarifying request.
lines: Notice on the twenty-eighth of April; today's date; that puts us inside the window with days to spare, but not many.
Murrow. Do we file the motion before or after the hearing time on Friday?

states._comment: Beat 9 — after Murrow's third-clause narration (third-party non-cure: the current renter is not Halina's agent). Cula registers the preempted defense and stops. The legal-argument register is doing the work; he does not over-comment. One line.
lines: Good. Then 'they accepted the notice' fails on its face. The current renter at number seven is a stranger to her, not an agent.

states._comment: Promotion 2026-05-17: collapsed dwell trio ['cula_b9_dwell_how_found', 'cula_b9_dwell_used_before', 'cula_b9_dwell_next'] into options block per Piotr's choice (a). Successor reply states gate on chapter1.state_choice and clear it on dismiss. Editorial note: Cula's dialogue file is only walked by family-photo interactable; these dwells are unreachable until engineering fan-out into NPC files.

Beat 9 dwell prompt — *How did you find this article?* Asked of Murrow; expected reply surfaces archival depth without Ch4 ledger content. One line. | Promotion 2026-05-17: reply leg of collapsed dwell trio; clears state_choice on dismiss.
lines: Murrow. The binder has hundreds of pages. How did you find the article that fits?

states._comment: Beat 9 dwell prompt — *Have you used this clause before?* Expected NPC reply: once, several years ago; brief reference. Cula's line is collegial. One line. | Promotion 2026-05-17: reply leg of collapsed dwell trio; clears state_choice on dismiss.
lines: Have you actually run a one-thirty-five-bis motion before, or is this our first?

states._comment: Beat 9 dwell prompt — *What do we need next?* Bridges to Beat 10 readiness check. One line. | Promotion 2026-05-17: reply leg of collapsed dwell trio; clears state_choice on dismiss.
lines: Murrow. What is on the checklist between now and Friday?

states._comment: Beat 9 close — case theory crystallized. Cula names the SHAPE of the argument out loud for the player; the legal-argument register lands cleanly. Per voice_agents/cula.md §Arc-aware notes for Chapter 1, this is one of the first places his legal-argument register lands. One line.
lines: Defective service under Article 135-bis. Motion to set aside, within fourteen days of actual notice, current resident not an authorised agent. That is a case.

states._comment: Beat 9 — THE Kundera interior step-back beat for Chapter 1. Used ONCE per chapter per voice_agents/cula.md §Patterns. Placement: after the case theory crystallises and before the readiness check. The 'I notice that I do not yet know whether I'm staying' canonical line is one option; this draft uses a Beat-9-specific variant on the same construction. The double-step ('I notice that I do not yet know') gives the essayist remove. Tagged 'internal' and once_per_chapter so a future runner can gate the second use.
states.speaker: cula_internal
lines: I notice that I do not yet know whether the firm is teaching me, or whether the case is teaching me, and that on a Friday this will not matter.

states._comment: Beat 12 Round 1 — defective service argument. Cula's court register: longer sentences, article number lands, no nervousness. Per voice_agents/cula.md §Co-stars (with judges): 'He delivers the article number and stops.' The canonical voice-reference court_001 line is the calibration. He addresses the judge with full court formality ('Wysoki Sądzie' / 'Your Honor' per address-form rules; the English draft uses 'Your Honor'). Two lines.
lines: Your Honor. The notice was served to a non-current address. The building was renumbered in two thousand and fifteen with the landlord's countersignature; the address used was not the address of the tenant. That is not service under Article 135-bis, paragraph two.
The client should not be treated as silent where the process failed to give her a real chance to speak.

states._comment: Beat 12 Round 2 — fair hearing argument. Whimsy carries the round; Cula's role is to land the procedural anchor that supports Whimsy's flourish. The voice-reference court_003 line ('Procedure is not decoration; it is the doorway to being heard') is calibration. One line — Cula gives Whimsy the floor.
lines: Your Honor. A party cannot meaningfully defend a case it was never properly brought into. Procedure is not decoration; it is the doorway to being heard.

states._comment: Beat 12 Round 3 — REMEDY line. Per chapter_1.md §306: flavor varies slightly by stance carrier. Sympathetic carries slight care. The remedy itself is unchanged — modest procedural reset, not total victory. Per voice_agents/cula.md §Arc-aware Chapter 1: legal-argument register first lands in District Court rounds. Two lines. The second line is the canonical remedy ask; the first carries the care.
lines: Your Honor. The defect was a year in the making, but its effect on Mrs. Sikorska's life was a single misdirected envelope.
We do not ask the court to decide the whole dispute today. We ask that the hearing be set aside and rescheduled, so that the client may enter the dispute at all.

states._comment: Beat 12 Round 3 — REMEDY line. Blunt-procedural carries pure procedure: no flourish, no biographical reach. The remedy itself is unchanged. Two lines, structural. Sentences in canonical Cula legal-argument register: longer, steadier, article number at the seam.
lines: Your Honor. The defects are sequential. Service was invalid under Article 135-bis. Actual notice triggered a fourteen-day window. The motion is timely; the third-party acceptance is not a cure.
On those grounds the hearing should be set aside and rescheduled.

states._comment: Beat 12 Round 3 — REMEDY line. Technical carries archival weight: the 1962 lease and 1987 inheritance ground the tenancy validity that supports the rehearing-remedy ask. Per chapter_1.md §306. Two lines. The remedy ask is the canon; the carrier is the historical anchor.
lines: Your Honor. The tenancy is documented from nineteen sixty-two, original lease in the father's name, and continuous since nineteen eighty-seven by inheritance. The address that the landlord did not use is the address that has held that tenancy for thirty-nine years.
On those grounds the hearing should be set aside and rescheduled so that the substance of the tenancy can be heard properly.

states._comment: Beat 13 — Cula's reaction on return to the office after the court win. The voice-reference victory_001 line ('We won the part of the case that allows the case to exist') is the calibration anchor. He does not celebrate; he registers the remedy precisely. One line. The dry-observer note ('appropriately legal for a first day') is the small swerve at the end.
lines: We won the part of the case that allows the case to exist. That feels appropriately legal for a first day.

states._comment: Beat 13 — Murrow's silent ledger update (drawer open, ledger out, brief notation, ledger back; no dialogue per beat sheet). Cula's reaction MUST also be silent. Per voice_agents/cula.md §Will not say: 'Any reference to the chapter-4 ledger glimpse, ever, in dialogue.' Chapter 1 is too early — the ledger has not yet acquired the spine weight. The silent:true state is a deliberate-empty marker so the writer's pack records the visual beat. No on_dismiss. No lines.
states.speaker: cula_internal

idle_flavor: Pig & Swine. Attorneys, counselors, possibly open. That is not how they described it at university.
No professional reason to flee yet. I will treat that as encouragement.
